message:
feat(btrfs): add cycle and duplicate-node detection to tree walker

Harden btrfs tree traversal against corrupt or malicious filesystem images
that contain pointer cycles or duplicate node references. Previously,
walk_tree/walk_node followed internal key pointers without any visited-node
tracking, which could cause unbounded recursion and stack overflow on
corrupt trees.

Changes:
- Add active_path HashSet to detect ancestor cycles during recursion
  (a node pointing back to itself or an ancestor in the current path)
- Add visited_nodes HashSet to detect non-cyclic duplicate references
  (same child node reached via different parents, causing repeated work
  and potentially incorrect duplicate results)
- Return ParseError::InvalidField with distinct reason strings for each
  case: "cycle detected" vs "duplicate node reference"
- Active path entries are removed on backtrack so sibling subtrees can
  still reference their own distinct nodes

Tests added:
- walk_self_cycle_fails_fast: root node points to itself
- walk_two_node_cycle_fails_fast: A -> B -> A cycle
- walk_duplicate_child_reference_fails_fast: root has two children
  pointing to the same leaf

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
